DISCOVERY 610, THE COURT HAS READ AND CONSIDERED THE LATE-FILED OPPOSITION. THE MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORY 17.1 AS TO REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS 1, 17, 22, 24, 25, 29, 30, 34, 41 - 44 IS GRANTED. IN ADDITION, THE MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORY 17.1 AS TO REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS: 3, 5 - 10, 15, 16, 18 - 21, 23, 26 - 28, 33, 35 - 37, 39, AND 40 IS GRANTED AS THE RESPONSES ARE NOT UNQUALIFIED ADMISSIONS. THE MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TO INTERROGATORIES: 10, 35, 36, 51, AND 55. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO THE REMAINING INTERROGATORIES. AS TO THOSE QUESTIONS THAT ASK TO IDENTIFY SPECIFIED WITNESSES, IF PLAINTIFF LACKS SUFFICIENT KNOWLEDGE, ANY RESPONSE SHALL COMPLY WITH CCP SECTION 2030.220(C). VERIFIED SUPPLEMENTAL RESPONSES SHALL BE SERVED WITHIN 30 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. THE COURT FINDS THAT PLAINTIFF MADE EVASIVE ANSWERS TO THOSE INTERROGATORIES SEEKING FACTS IN SUPPORT OF CERTAIN CONTENTIONS BY STATING CONCLUSIONS ONLY. MONETARY SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,190 SHALL BE PAID WITHIN 30 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. JUDGE: BRUCE CHAN. NOT REPORTED.

(Re-Notice) Of Motion And Motion Of Dick W. Wong Dba Dick W. Wong Associates To Ocmpel Pltf To Provide Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One, Special Interrogatories, Set One, And Code-Complaint Verifications; And Request For Monetary And/Or Issue Sanctions
Off Calendar Feb-25-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: TENTATIVE RULING WAS ADOPTED AS FOLLOWSW: The Court has read and considered the late-filed opposition. The motion to compel further responses to form interrogatory 17.1 as to requests for admissions 1, 17, 22, 24, 25, 29, 30, 34, 41 - 44 is granted. In addition, the motion to compel responses to form interrogatory 17.1 as to requests for admissions: 3, 5 - 10, 15, 16, 18 - 21, 23, 26 - 28, 33, 35 - 37, 39, and 40 is granted as the responses are not unqualified admissions. The motion to compel further responses to special interrogatories is granted in part and denied in part. The motion is denied as to interrogatories: 10, 35, 36, 51, and 55. The motion is granted as to the remaining interrogatories. As to those questions that ask to identify specified witnesses, if plaintiff lacks sufficient knowledge, any response shall comply with CCP Section 2030.220(c). Verified supplemental responses shall be served within 30 days of notice of this order. The Court finds that plaintiff made evasive answers to those interrogatories seeking facts in support of certain contentions by stating conclusions only. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $1,190 shall be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. Judge: Bruce Chan. Not reported. (610/BEC)
